title: stelestylis surinamensis
intro: Stelestylis surinamensis, also known as the "Guianese steeple-cheddar," is a type of flowering plant native to the Guiana Shield in South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Stelestylis surinamensis has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and has been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
